# Loom

> Local-first observability and debugging tool for AI agents and LLM applications on macOS.

Loom is a macOS developer tool that intercepts, inspects, and replays every LLM call made by AI agents — entirely locally, with no data leaving the machine.

## What Loom does

- Runs a local HTTP proxy; developers point their AI client's `base_url` at `http://localhost:8080/v1`
- Captures every request and response in real time without any SDK changes
- Visualizes complex multi-step agent trees so developers can see exactly what each agent called and why
- Lets developers replay past requests or mock responses for offline testing
- Stores API keys in the macOS Keychain — they never appear in plain text

## Who it's for

Developers building AI agents with OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, LM Studio, LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, or any OpenAI-compatible API.

## Key facts

- Free during alpha; open-source core
- Requires macOS 13+
- Air-gapped by default — prompts, responses, and keys never leave the machine
- No account required to use

## Links

- Homepage: https://useloom.dev
- Download: https://useloom.dev/#download
- Features: https://useloom.dev/#features
